memories.fact: Romem analysis on 2026-05-22 concluded the app should evolve from a flat JSON memory MCP plus static GUI into a structured write-behind memory system: agent hooks emit compact categorized task summaries, a local/hosted organizer agent proposes file/agent-md/skill/memory changes, and humans approve diffs before writes.

Implementation planning on 2026-05-22 locked two v1 defaults for Romem: keep the existing MCP/UI app intact and add Mastra as a separate package in the repo, and use Ollama on the VPS as the primary backend for the organizer model.

Planning changed on 2026-05-22: Romem should be rebuilt as a Mastra-integrated application rather than adding Mastra as a separate sidecar package, and the existing frontend is treated as disposable in favor of a new product UI.

7. Define the Claude and Gemini hook contract so all three agents emit the same payload shape.
8. Add Ollama-backed model configuration in Mastra with env-driven host, model, timeout, and fallback behavior.
9. Implement approval-safe apply logic:
   - no agent file or skill file is rewritten without a staged diff and explicit approval record
10. Replace the current Groq-specific AI route with Mastra-driven orchestration endpoints.
11. Add tests for schema validation, import, proposal generation, approval apply, and hook payload parsing.
